rules: NEVER modify any remote theme. `larke/main` exists for the user only.
Work exclusively against the local `shopify theme dev` server.

export.note: WebP Exporter is a Figma PLUGIN and cannot be driven by MCP. Reproduce it: download_assets PNG @3x, then `cwebp -q 90` locally (cwebp 1.6.0 is installed).

browser.note: The ONLY stable identifier is device_id. The extension's display names ('Browser 1'/'Browser 2') are positional and DO shift between sessions — they swapped between the 2026-07-13 run and the next one. Never select a browser by name. This device is the Chrome logged into the wearelarke admin. The other connected device sits behind a /challenges/user_verification bot-detection page: never solve or bypass it.
browser.selected_by: main loop, before the workflow starts — subagents must NOT call list_connected_browsers, select_browser or switch_browser (they cannot prompt the user, and switch_browser times out). The selection is already active; just call tabs_context_mcp.

qa.note: Pixel diff is ONLY allowed at 1440 (Desktop artboard) and 375 (Mobile artboard) — the two widths that have a real Figma reference. At 1024/768 there is NO ground truth: run metric + sanity checks only (no horizontal scroll, no overlap, no clipped text, touch targets >= 44px). Never invent a reference; a fabricated 'pass' is worse than no check.

naming.note: Several Figma frames carry no semantic name (Frame 4, Frame 6564, four different 'Image and text'). The PM inspects each via get_screenshot and assigns a descriptive kebab-case name from what the section actually shows. Record the chosen name back into this file so it is stable across runs.

build_order: 1. Shared chrome first — announcement-bar, site-header, promo-bar, site-footer appear on EVERY page. Build once; never re-implement per page.
4. Content pages (Our Story, Tree Fibre, Delivery & Returns, Contact, Shipping).

sections.name: brand-social-gallery
sections.note: Built as dev-brand-social-gallery. User's dev link points at the inner content frame 45031:2811 (Frame 6565, 1440x236.75); 45031:2809 is the full section incl. the 80px top spacer — use it for the full-section pixel diff. Badge image on the homepage is shopify://shop_images/group_6568.webp (green Larke disc inside a magenta→gold gradient ring). Text-column order is heading → badge → handle.

sections.name: sleep-grows-on-trees
sections.note: Part 1 of 3 carved out of Figma frame 45071:2617, which bundles the cards, the CTA and the guarantee strip into one frame. Built as three sections.

sections.name: naturally-best-sleep
sections.note: Part 2 of 3 of frame 45071:2617. Outlined button variant, not the solid green one from fibre-cta.

sections.name: pdp-guarantees
sections.note: Part 3 of 3 of frame 45071:2617. NOT dev-trust-badges: no icons, and it nests a Cream/50 tile inside a Cream/100 panel.

our_story.note: Enumerated from the artboard 2026-07-13. This list was EMPTY before, which is why 'Values we Cherish.' was silently never built — nobody had broken the page into sections. Keep it filled.

sections.name: our-story-hero
sections.built_as: dev-tree-fibre-hero with show_underline (same _Master Hero Media component)

components.note: Drawers/modals, not page sections. Build after the pages they belong to.

figma_quirks: Mobile 'Frame 3' (44821:902) is only a WRAPPER around Frame 6564 + Video section + Footer. Do not treat it as a section.

RESOLVED (2026-07-13): Mobile Homepage shows two 'Menu - Mobile' nodes, but they are the SAME component — 45240:2518 is the component MASTER (symbol, 375x66) and 45015:873 is an INSTANCE of it placed on the page at y=36 (375x65.02). Neither is a stray duplicate; both render identically (hamburger / Larke wordmark / bag). Build and diff against the master, 45240:2518. The earlier note here claimed 45015:873 was a stray duplicate — that was wrong.
'Promo Bar - mobile' is the layer name on the DESKTOP page too — the name is misleading, it is not mobile-only.
Desktop has two frames both named 'Shipping' (45246:3849, 45246:4413). Only 45246:3849 is mapped.
instructions: Larke — UK duvet/bedding brand. Store handle: wearelarke. Never touch any remote theme. Images: download_assets PNG@3x -> cwebp -q 90 -> upload to Shopify Files via the Chrome extension. No API tokens anywhere, ever.
